ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for sourdough cake; mapped to generic cake food product as closest defensible ancestor. Sourdough-specific and Malaysian-style variants are not individually coded in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_description: Bread and similar products — used as closest FoodEx2 anchor for sourdough-based baked cake; no dedicated sourdough cake code exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated sourdough cake entry. A000N (bread and similar products) is the closest defensible parent node.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for sourdough cake as a specific food concept; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 4 pieces of golden sourdough cake, weighing approximately 200 g, as served at EC Coffee Bar.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Cake, coffeecake, cinnamon with crumb topping, commercially prepared, enriched (FDC ID 168869) and Bread, sourdough (FDC ID 325871), used as composite reference
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry exists for Malaysian-style golden sourdough cake. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central coffeecake and sourdough bread entries as the closest approved-source analogues. Input per_100g values (energy 325 kcal, protein 6 g, fat 17.5 g, carbohydrate 35 g, sugar 17.5 g, sodium 150 mg, dietary fibre 1.5 g, saturated fat 10 g, trans fat 0.25 g, cholesterol 75 mg, calcium 40 mg, iron 0.75 mg) are consistent with USDA coffeecake reference ranges and are retained with medium confidence. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, and micronutrients not reported in the input are set to -1 where not available from the analogous USDA source.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Cake (plain, made from packet mix) GI ~65 used as analogous estimate for sourdough-based butter cake.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Malaysian-style golden sourdough cake. GI of 65 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for plain cake (made from packet mix, GI ~65), which is the closest approved analogous food. Sourdough fermentation may lower GI slightly, but the high sugar content (17.5 g/100g) and butter content offset this effect. GL per serving calculated as: GI × (carbohydrate per serving in g) / 100 = 65 × (35 × 2.0) / 100 = 65 × 70 / 100 = 45.5.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (17.5 g/100g) with low dietary fibre (1.5 g/100g) drives insulin-mediated sodium retention; saturated fat (10 g/100g) contributes to arterial stiffness.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption to no more than one piece per occasion, as the high sugar load (17.5 g/100g) promotes insulin-driven sodium retention and the saturated fat content (10 g/100g) contributes to vascular stiffness, both of which can elevate blood pressure independently of dietary sodium.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar at 17.5 g/100g exceeds the 15 g/100g threshold; dietary fibre at 1.5 g/100g is below the 2 g/100g minimum; estimated GI of 65 (medium) combined with a glycemic load of 45.5 per serving indicates a high glycemic impact per eating occasion.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this food: the high sugar content (17.5 g/100g), low fibre (1.5 g/100g), and estimated glycemic load of 45.5 per 200 g serving will cause rapid postprandial glucose elevation, and the saturated fat (10 g/100g) from butter worsens insulin resistance with regular consumption.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 10 g/100g (50% of total fat) substantially exceeds the AHA recommended limit; trans fat at 0.25 g/100g and cholesterol at 75 mg/100g add further atherogenic risk; high sugar (17.5 g/100g) raises triglycerides.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption of this food, as the saturated fat content (10 g/100g) and trans fat (0.25 g/100g) raise LDL cholesterol, the high sugar (17.5 g/100g) elevates triglycerides, and the combined effect significantly increases atherosclerotic cardiovascular risk per AHA 2021 dietary guidelines.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat at 10 g/100g (butter-derived) exceeds the 5 g/100g pro-inflammatory threshold; sugar at 17.5 g/100g exceeds the 15 g/100g pro-inflammatory threshold; no anti-inflammatory components present.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this food: butter-derived saturated fat (10 g/100g) promotes TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory cytokine activity, and sugar at 17.5 g/100g further drives systemic inflammation, with no omega-3, polyphenol, or anti-inflammatory component present to offset these effects.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Golden Sourdough Cake (4 pieces) is a Malaysian-inspired baked dessert cake served at EC Coffee Bar in Singapore, presented as four individual pieces with a combined serving weight of approximately 200 g. The cake is built on a sourdough-fermented wheat flour base, giving it a mildly tangy flavour profile that contrasts with its rich, buttery sweetness. The texture is soft and moist with a golden-coloured crumb, characteristic of a butter-enriched coffee cake style. Key ingredients include sourdough starter, wheat flour, butter, sugar, and eggs. Per 100 g, the cake provides 325 kcal, 6 g protein, 17.5 g total fat (of which 10 g is saturated fat from butter), 35 g carbohydrate, and 17.5 g sugar, with only 1.5 g dietary fibre. The sodium content is moderate at 150 mg/100g. The estimated glycemic index is 65 (medium category), but the glycemic load per 200 g serving is high at approximately 45.5, making it a significant glucose challenge per eating occasion. Clinically, this food is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to high sugar and low fibre, unsuitable for those with cardiovascular disease due to high saturated fat and trans fat, and should be consumed with caution by individuals managing hypertension or rheumatoid arthritis. It is suitable for vegetarians and is likely halal. The sourdough fermentation process provides minimal glycemic benefit given the high sugar and butter content. This food is best enjoyed as an occasional treat rather than a regular dietary component.
common_user_queries: How many calories in golden sourdough 4 pieces?
Is EC Coffee Bar sourdough cake suitable for hypertension?
What is the glycemic index of sourdough cake?
How much sugar is in sourdough cake?
Is sourdough cake good for heart health?

confidence_reason: No exact approved-source entry exists for Malaysian-style golden sourdough cake. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central coffeecake and sourdough bread analogues, which are consistent with the input values provided. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database using plain cake as the closest analogous food. Confidence is medium because the food is a restaurant-specific preparation without a direct primary-source match.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this Malaysian-style sourdough cake preparation is not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2024. potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g set to -1: these micronutrient and moisture values are not reported in the analogous USDA coffeecake reference and are not available from an approved source for this specific preparation. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero — baked butter cake with no fruit ingredients contains negligible vitamin C; consistent with USDA FoodData Central coffeecake reference. trans_fat_g of 0.25 g/100g is retained from input; consistent with butter-containing baked goods per USDA reference. Nutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central coffeecake (FDC ID 168869) and sourdough bread (FDC ID 325871) as composite analogous reference. GI of 65 estimated from University of Sydney GI Database plain cake entry; glycemic load per 200 g serving = 45.5. Country of origin recorded as Singapore (restaurant location); primary cuisine influence is Malaysian. Halal status assumed based on vegetarian ingredients but not confirmed by certification.
